message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it from the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Find the code path/contract/test that makes the PoC impossible or moot. Only survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

SC-2: Operator-username PII not redacted from transcripts when home-dir name is short (<4 chars) or appears in dash-delimited project slug
File: scripts/capture_transcripts.py:67
Description: _scrub redacts the operator's username two ways: the path rule `(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s"']+` (requires literal '/Users/[REDACTED]') and _USERNAME_RE derived from Path.home().name. The Claude project-dir slug that DEFAULT_SRC points at is dash-delimited (e.g. '-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc'); the path rule does NOT match it (no slashes), so the only thing masking the username inside slugs, git-author strings, and prose is _USERNAME_RE. But _username_pattern() returns None for any home-dir name shorter than 4 chars (to avoid over-redacting common words). For operators whose login is <4 chars (e.g. 'bob','amy','ci', 'root' is also skipped), the username then leaks verbatim into committed transcripts via the slug and any prose mention — defeating the stated #F-024/#G-007 PII-scrubbing goal for that class of operator. The capture script's own docstring claims credential-shaped secrets AND operator PII are redacted, so this is an undisclosed completeness gap rather than documented honest scope.
PoC: Set HOME so Path.home().name='bob'; create $SRC=.../-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc/<id>.jsonl containing the slug path and an author line 'bob'. Run capture_transcripts (or commit via the pre-commit hook). The committed transcripts/sessions/<id>.jsonl still contains 'bob' and '-Users-[REDACTED]-...' unredacted because _USERNAME_RE is None (len<4) and the '/Users/' path rule never matches the dash slug.

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED] _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
56	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
57	
58	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
59	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
60	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
61	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
62	    """
63	    try:
64	        name = Path.home().name
65	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
66	        return None
67	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
68	        return None
69	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
70	
71	
72	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
73	
74	
75	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
76	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
77	        if pat.groups == 2:
78	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
79	        elif pat.groups == 1:
80	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
81	        else:
82	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
83	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
84	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
85	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
86	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
87	    return text
88	
89	
90	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
91	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
92	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
93	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
94	
95	
96	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
97	    try:
98	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
99	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
100	        if srcf.suffix == ".jsonl":
101	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
102	        else:
103	            data = raw
104	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
105	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
106	        else:
107	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
108	        return {
109	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
110	            "bytes": len(data),
111	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
112	        }
113	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
114	        return None
115	
116	
117	def main() -> int:
118	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
119	    if not src.is_dir():
120	        return 0
121	    session = _active_session(src)
122	    if session is None:
123	        return 0
124	    sid = session.stem
125	    captured: list[dict] = []
126	
127	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
128	    if rec:
129	        captured.append(rec)
130	
131	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
132	    subtree = src / sid
133	    if subtree.is_dir():
134	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
135	            if f.is_file():
136	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
137	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
138	                if rec:
139	                    captured.append(rec)
140	
141	    try:
142	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
143	        MANIFEST.write_text(
144	            json.dumps(
145	                {
146	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
147	                    "active_session": sid,
148	                    "n_files": len(captured),
149	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
150	                    "files": captured,
151	                },
152	                indent=2,
153	            )
154	            + "\n",
155	            encoding="utf-8",
156	        )
157	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
158	        return 0
159	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
160	    return 0
161	
162	
163	if __name__ == "__main__":
164	    sys.exit(main())
165

cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && grep -rn "capture_transcripts\|_username_pattern\|_USERNAME_RE\|_scrub" tests/ 2>/dev/null | head -50; echo "---HOOK---"; cat .githooks/pre-commit 2>/dev/null | head -40

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Provenance: snapshot the active Claude session transcript(s) into transcripts/ on every commit
# so each commit carries an auditable record of the agent interactions/effort behind it.
# Best-effort — never block a commit.
set -u
ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" || exit 0
PY="$ROOT/.venv/bin/python"
[ -x "$PY" ] || PY="$(command -v python3 || true)"
[ -n "$PY" ] || exit 0
"$PY" "$ROOT/scripts/capture_transcripts.py" 2>/dev/null || true
git add -- "$ROOT/transcripts" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0

61	        "license_gate left the report file open (#F-050)"
62	    )
63	
64	
65	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_operator_pii():
66	    # #F-024 (mandate-compatible mitigation): transcripts are committed as provenance per the
67	    # campaign mandate, but the capture scrubber must mask operator PII — email addresses and the
68	    # home-path username — so identity is not published. The path STRUCTURE is preserved.
69	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_pii")
70	    out = cap._scrub("user [REDACTED] at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py and /home/[REDACTED]/y")
71	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out
72	    assert "somebody" not in out
73	    assert "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py" in out  # structure kept, username masked
74	    assert "/home/[REDACTED]/y" in out
75	
76	
77	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username():
78	    # #G-007: the operator username must be redacted wherever it appears (git author strings,
79	    # project-dir slugs like -Users-[REDACTED]-..., prose), not only inside /Users|/home paths.
80	    from pathlib import Path
81	
82	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_user")
83	    name = Path.home().name
84	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
85	        import pytest
86	
87	        pytest.skip("home username too short to redact safely")
88	    text = f"git author: {name} <x@y.z>; project dir -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
89	    out = cap._scrub(text)
90	    assert name not in out  # bare handle masked everywhere, not only in a /Users path
91	
92

content.text:
The finding is fully confirmed by the code and even by the test suite, which itself skips when the username is `<4` chars.

Verifying the PoC mechanics:

1. `_username_pattern()` (line 67): returns `None` when `len(name) < 4`. So for `bob` (3 chars), `amy` (3), `ci` (2), `_USERNAME_RE` is `None`.
2. `_scrub` (lines 85-86): the bare-username redaction only runs `if _USERNAME_RE is not None`. With it `None`, no bare-username masking occurs.
3. The path rule `(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s"']+` (line 51) requires literal `/Users/[REDACTED] or `/home/[REDACTED] with slashes. The Claude project-dir slug is dash-delimited (`-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc`) — no slashes — so this rule does NOT match it. Confirmed: `DEFAULT_SRC` points at `-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc` (line 29), a dash slug.
4. Therefore for a `<4`-char login: the slug `-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc`, git-author `bob`, and any prose `bob` leak verbatim into committed `transcripts/sessions/<id>.jsonl`.

The test `test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username` even `pytest.skip`s exactly this case (`len(name) < 4`), so there is no test coverage that would catch the leak — it is an acknowledged, untested gap. The docstring (lines 9, 34-37) claims operator PII / username-in-paths are redacted, with no carve-out for short usernames, making this an undisclosed completeness gap.

I could not find any other code path, contract, or test that closes this gap. The PoC holds.

input.reasoning: Confirmed from the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py. (1) _username_pattern() (lines 64-68) returns None whenever len(Path.home().name) < 4, so for logins like 'bob','amy','ci' _USERNAME_RE is None. (2) In _scrub, the bare-username redaction is gated by `if _USERNAME_RE is not None` (lines 85-86), so it never runs for short logins. (3) The path rule re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+") (line 51) requires literal slashes around the name; the Claude project-dir slug is dash-delimited (DEFAULT_SRC = '-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc', line 29), which the rule cannot match. Result: for a <4-char operator login, the username leaks verbatim via the dash slug (-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc), git-author strings, and prose into committed transcripts/sessions/<id>.jsonl. The docstring (lines 9, 11-13, 34-37) and #G-007 claim operator PII / home-path username are redacted with no documented carve-out for short names, so this is an undisclosed completeness gap. Notably, the project's own test test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username (tests/test_scripts.py:77-90) explicitly pytest.skips when len(name) < 4, so the leaking class of operator is not covered by any test. No other code path, contract, or test disproves the PoC.

content.text: The finding survives. I could not disprove it — the code at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py` confirms the gap exactly, and the project's own test even skips the vulnerable case (`len(name) < 4`).
